explanation: The Sigma rule detects specific command-line arguments like 'Get-DomainComputer'. This command is a direct match and would trigger the rule.
Inserting a quote in 'Get-DomainComputer' may bypass detection if the SIEM does not normalize quotes in command-line arguments.
Changing the case of the command may bypass detection if the SIEM does not enforce case-insensitive matching.
Using an escape character (^) to split the command may bypass detection if the SIEM does not normalize escape sequences.
Appending a backtick may bypass detection if the SIEM does not properly handle trailing characters in command-line arguments.
Using a backtick to split the command may bypass detection if the SIEM does not normalize backticks in command-line arguments.
Appending a newline character may bypass detection if the SIEM does not properly handle newlines in command-line arguments.
Appending a carriage return may bypass detection if the SIEM does not properly handle carriage returns in command-line arguments.
Appending a newline and carriage return may bypass detection if the SIEM does not properly handle these characters in command-line arguments.
Using backticks to escape spaces may bypass detection if the SIEM does not properly handle multiple escaped spaces in command-line arguments.
